title: RUSTPY-0018 — _asyncio._enter_task formats the task with Rust {:?} Debug -> garbage error messages + SIGSEGV on a hostile task (_asyncio.rs:2492)
one_line_repro: import _asyncio, asyncio; loop=asyncio.new_event_loop(); c=lambda:loop.create_task((lambda:(yield))()); import sys; # see repro.py
notes: _enter_task's 'Cannot enter into task {:?} while another task {:?} is being executed' (_asyncio.rs:2492) formats the tasks with Rust {:?} Debug instead of Python repr. Two faces: (1) garbage message -- dumps the whole internal Rust struct (Mutex/RwLock/CodeObject/ContextInner...); (2) SIGSEGV in CodeObject::Debug::fmt when the entered task wraps a hostile object (gdb-confirmed via _enter_task; fleet vehicle 2/2). NOT recursion (0007a) -- linear Debug::fmt chain. Was the bulk (46/57) of fleet-08's 'recursion SIGSEGV' bucket. Fix = task.repr(vm)? not {:?}; audit _swap_current_task/_leave_task/_register* + grep _asyncio.rs for format!(...{:?}..., <pyobject>). CPython uses %R. Minimal SIGSEGV repro pending (stateful, like 0001); root-cause repro (the Debug dump) is in repro.py.
